title: Institute Settings
description: Manage your institute information, branding, and configuration.
noPermission: You do not have permission to edit this section.
refreshToSeeLatest: Someone else modified this. Refresh to see latest.
unsavedChanges: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?

info.codeDescription: Assigned during institute creation. Contact support to change.
info.typeDescription: Set during institute creation. Contact support to change the type.
info.title: Institute Information
info.name: Institute Name
info.departmentsDescription: Academic departments offered by this institute.
info.contactDescription: Phone numbers and email addresses for your institute.

phoneValidation.atLeastOnePrimary: At least one phone must be marked as primary.
phoneValidation.atLeastOneWhatsapp: At least one phone must be WhatsApp-enabled.
phoneValidation.invalidNumber: Phone number must be exactly 10 digits.

info.line1Description: Building name, house/plot number, and street.
info.line3Description: Any extra details that help locate the institute (optional).
info.cityDescription: City, town, or village where the institute is located.
info.districtDescription: Revenue district as per government records.
info.stateHelpBody: When your institute is affiliated to a state-specific board (e.g. HBSE, Bihar Board, Maharashtra SSC), only states in that board's jurisdiction are selectable. This prevents affiliation-address mismatches that block TC issuance and UDISE+ submission. To enable more states, update your Board Affiliations above or contact support.
info.postalCodeDescription: 6-digit Indian postal code. City, district, and state auto-fill on blur.
info.postalCodeValidation: PIN code must be exactly 6 digits.
info.postalCodeHelp: Enter the 6-digit PIN code assigned by India Post. We auto-fill the city, district, and state from it — you can still edit them afterwards.
info.pinLookupNotFound: Could not auto-fill from this PIN code. Please enter the city, district, and state manually.
info.coordinatesHelp: Latitude and longitude are optional. They are used for maps and geofenced attendance. Get them from Google Maps by right-clicking the institute location.
info.identifiersDescription: UDISE, registration numbers, and other regulatory identifiers.
info.affiliationsDescription: Affiliations with education boards (CBSE, ICSE, state boards, etc.).

fieldHelp.identifiersBody: Government-issued IDs that link your institute to official reporting systems. Different types unlock different features — UDISE+ drives census reports, board registrations appear on Transfer Certificates, and registration numbers are printed on receipts for tax purposes.
fieldHelp.identifiersTypes: Common types: UDISE+ — 11-digit Ministry of Education code. State Registration — society or trust registration number. CBSE/ICSE School Code — affiliation-issued institute code. GSTIN — 15-character tax ID. PAN — 10-character income tax ID. Shops & Establishment — local municipal licence.
fieldHelp.affiliationsBody: Recognitions from education boards that govern the curriculum, exams, and certifications your institute can offer. An institute can hold multiple affiliations simultaneously — e.g. CBSE for classes 6-12 plus State Board for grades 1-5 — but only one can be marked primary.
fieldHelp.affiliationStatusOptions: Active — currently recognised and in good standing. Provisional — granted but subject to further inspection (common for new schools). Expired — past the validity date; students already enrolled continue under legacy rules. Withdrawn — the board revoked affiliation, usually for compliance breaches.
fieldHelp.affiliationGrantedLevelBody: Highest class the board permits you to run. A 'Secondary' grant covers up to Class 10; 'Senior Secondary' extends to Class 12. Running classes beyond your granted level is a compliance breach — the board can withdraw affiliation.

branding.title: Branding
branding.logoDescription: Institute logo displayed in the header and reports. Recommended: 256×256px, PNG or SVG.
branding.logoUrlDescription: URL of your institute's logo. Recommended: 256×256px, PNG or SVG.
branding.faviconDescription: Browser tab icon. Recommended: 32×32px, PNG or ICO.
branding.faviconUrlDescription: URL of the browser tab icon. Recommended: 32×32px, PNG or ICO.
branding.primaryColorDescription: Main accent colour used across the interface.
branding.secondaryColorDescription: Supporting colour for backgrounds and highlights.
branding.themeDescription: Visual theme preset for the institute portal.
branding.coverImageDescription: Displayed on the login page and public profile. Recommended: 1920×480px.
branding.coverImageUrlDescription: URL of the cover image shown on the login page. Recommended: 1920×480px.
branding.previewDescription: Live preview of your branding changes.
branding.updatedByOther: Branding was updated by another admin
branding.colorValidation: Must be a valid hex colour (e.g. #1A2B3C).

fieldHelp.logoBody: Used in the app header, PDF report cards, marksheets, Transfer Certificates, fee receipts, and ID cards. Must be legible at 24px (tab header) and 200px+ (letterhead). Upload a square PNG or SVG with transparent background — JPGs with white backgrounds look bad in dark mode.
fieldHelp.logoExample: Recommended: 256×256 px, under 200 KB. If your official logo is rectangular, crop to the icon-only version for the header and keep the full version for the cover image.
fieldHelp.faviconBody: The tiny icon shown in browser tabs and bookmarks. Browsers cache favicons aggressively — parents may continue seeing the old one for a few days after you change it.
fieldHelp.primaryColorBody: Used for buttons, links, active nav items, and the login page accent. Pick a colour with strong contrast on white (WCAG AA — contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1) otherwise text on buttons will be hard to read for parents with vision impairments.
fieldHelp.primaryColorExample: Good: #0F5132 (dark green), #1F3B8A (navy), #7F1D1D (maroon). Avoid pastels and light yellows — they fail contrast.
fieldHelp.themeBody: Preset look-and-feel for your portal. Only affects layout styling — your logo and colours always apply on top. Classic suits traditional K-12 schools; Modern suits CBSE/IB schools; Minimal suits coaching institutes; Default is a balanced middle ground.
fieldHelp.coverImageBody: The wide banner shown behind the login form and on your institute's public profile. Use a real photograph of your campus, labs, or events — generic stock images reduce trust. Avoid text in the image; any text you add will not translate to Hindi for Hindi users.

config.title: Configuration
config.attendanceTypeDescription: How attendance is recorded — once per day or per lecture/period.
config.shiftsDescription: Define shift timings if the institute operates multiple shifts.
config.gradingSystemDescription: Set by the affiliated board. Contact support to change.
config.termStructureDescription: Academic terms/semesters for the current year.
config.sectionStrengthDescription: Default student capacity rules for sections.
config.optimalStrengthDescription: Ideal number of students per section.
config.hardMaxDescription: Absolute cap — no section can exceed this.
config.exemptionAllowedDescription: Permit exceeding the hard max with admin approval.
config.updatedByOther: Configuration was updated by another admin

fieldHelp.attendanceTypeBody: Whether attendance is captured once per day or per teaching period. Affects which timetables show attendance links, how fortnightly/monthly reports are computed, and whether students can be marked present but absent for specific lectures.
fieldHelp.attendanceTypeDaily: Daily — a single present/absent mark per student per day. Best for primary schools and boarding institutions where students stay on campus all day.
fieldHelp.attendanceTypeLectureWise: Lecture-wise — one mark per student per period. Needed for senior secondary, coaching institutes, and colleges where students attend discrete subjects with different teachers.
fieldHelp.gradingSystemBody: Controls the grade bands shown on report cards, marksheets, and TCs. Locked by board affiliation — CBSE uses CGPA 1–10, ICSE uses percentage + grade, state boards often use letter grades. Changing this mid-session breaks historical marksheets, so it is managed by support.
fieldHelp.optimalStrengthBody: The target number of students per section under your teaching policy. Used for capacity planning — the Sections page warns when you are close to this number and lets you rebalance before the next admission. Below the RTE teacher-student ratio (30:1 up to Class 5, 35:1 for Classes 6-8) is strongly recommended.
fieldHelp.hardMaxBody: The absolute ceiling — no section can accept more students than this, even with admin override. Set in line with your board's norms: CBSE recommends 40, State Boards often allow 45, and the RTE Act caps primary sections at 30 where applicable. Enforced server-side to prevent over-admission.
fieldHelp.exemptionAllowedBody: When enabled, super-admins can temporarily exceed the hard maximum with a logged reason (RTE 25% admission, sibling joining mid-session, etc.). Each exemption is audited. Leave disabled for boards that do not permit over-capacity sections under any circumstances.
fieldHelp.shiftsBody: If your institute runs morning and afternoon shifts (common in urban government and aided schools), define each shift separately so timetables, fees, and attendance are scoped to the correct shift. Each student is assigned to exactly one shift per academic year.
